decks.notes: The main concept of this Modern Control - UrzaTron deck is to assemble the UrzaTron lands (Urza's Tower, Mine, and Power Plant) to produce massive amounts of colorless mana. This allows the deck to quickly ramp into powerful Eldrazi creatures such as Devourer of Destiny and Thought-Knot Seer, as well as hard-to-answer threats like Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, Eye of the Storms. The deck aims to disrupt opponents with cards like Chalice of the Void and Kozilek's Command while maintaining board control with removal spells like Dismember and All Is Dust.

This deck aims to quickly deploy cheap artifacts and build a board presence with powerful synergies. Emry and Tamiyo provide card advantage and recursion, while Quantum Riddler and Haywire Mite serve as threats. Mishra's Bauble and Mox Opal enable explosive starts, while various utility artifacts and spells provide disruption and removal. Urza's Saga is a key component, helping to search for important artifacts and generate additional value. The deck seeks to overwhelm opponents with its efficient creatures, disruption, and explosive potential.

This deck focuses on utilizing artifacts and synergies between them to quickly overwhelm opponents with a fast and aggressive strategy. It includes cards like Engineered Explosives and Metallic Rebuke to control the board, while also using cards like Arcbound Ravager and Pinnacle Emissary to generate powerful threats. With a mix of removal, disruption, and card advantage, this deck aims to establish a dominant board presence and maintain pressure throughout the game. Additionally, the inclusion of Urza's Saga provides additional value and versatility to further enhance the deck's capabilities.

The main focus of this Boros Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and efficient removal spells. The deck aims to establish a strong board presence early on with cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, while also providing support with cards like Galvanic Discharge and Thraben Charm. The deck utilizes powerful Planeswalkers like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and strong anthem effects like Ocelot Pride to boost the strength of its creatures. Additionally, the deck includes disruption in the form of Ranger-Captain of Eos and Blood Moon to control the opponent's resources.

The key concept of this Boros Aggro deck is to quickly establish a board presence with aggressive creatures like Guide of Souls and Seasoned Pyromancer, while also utilizing burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment to control the board and finish off the opponent. Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Voice of Victory provide additional support for the aggressive strategy, while cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Thraben Charm offer versatility in dealing with different threats. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Blood Moon to hinder opponents' strategies, making it a well-rounded aggressive deck for Modern 1 vs 1 gameplay.

The deck focuses on quickly deploying artifacts and utilizing them to generate card advantage and disruption while applying pressure on the opponent. Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student help recur artifacts and draw cards, while Quantum Riddler provides card selection. Haywire Mite and Cori-Steel Cutter serve as aggressive threats. Mishra's Bauble and Mox Opal enable fast mana production. Metallic Rebuke and Unholy Heat provide interaction and removal. Urza's Saga helps to tutor for key artifacts. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a mix of threats, disruption, and card advantage.

This deck focuses on using instant speed reanimation spells like Goryo's Vengeance and Ephemerate to cheat powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Solitude into play from the graveyard. Fallaji Archaeologist and Psychic Frog provide additional support and synergy with the strategy. The deck also includes disruption with cards like Thoughtseize and Force of Negation to protect the combo and control the board. The mana base is versatile to support the various color requirements of the deck. The goal is to quickly assemble the combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and control elements.

The deck aims to win through a combo that utilizes Hydroelectric Specimen and a high mana count. By quickly ramping up mana through cards like Lotus Bloom and Whir of Invention, the player can drop multiple threats in a single turn, overwhelming the opponent. Disruption cards like Disrupting Shoal and Jwari Disruption help to control the opponent's board while the combo is being set up. The inclusion of counterspells like Spell Snare and Flare of Denial provide protection for the combo pieces. Overall, it is a fast-paced and aggressive deck that aims to win quickly.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly ramp up mana with cards like Arboreal Grazer and Amulet of Vigor, allowing for explosive plays with Primeval Titan and Scapeshift to either overwhelm the opponent with powerful lands or quickly win the game. The deck also includes toolbox cards like Green Sun's Zenith and Urza's Saga to search for key pieces needed for the combo. With efficient land bouncing and ramping effects, this deck aims to assemble its combo pieces quickly and efficiently to dominate the game.

The deck aims to assemble a powerful combo using cards like Basking Broodscale, Glaring Fleshraker, and Devourer of Destiny to generate mana and creature tokens. This is combined with Eldrazi creatures like Emrakul, the Promised End and Sowing Mycospawn to overwhelm the opponent. The deck also includes disruption elements like Malevolent Rumble and Ugin, Eye of the Storms to control the board. Finally, the deck utilizes powerful lands like Urza's Saga and Eldrazi Temple to accelerate its game plan and achieve victory through a combination of aggression and control.

This deck focuses on quickly reanimating powerful creatures and using them to gain a significant advantage over the opponent. By combining cards like Goryo's Vengeance and Ephemerate with creatures like Griselbrand and Atraxa, Grand Unifier, the deck can generate a large amount of value in a short amount of time. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Psychic Frog and Thoughtseize to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Overall, the deck aims to assemble its combo pieces quickly and overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and disruptive spells.

This Jeskai Control deck focuses on utilizing a combination of powerful control spells and disruptive creatures to control the game and outvalue opponents. The deck includes a mix of removal, counter spells, and card draw to maintain board control and disrupt the opponent's game plan. By generating card advantage with cards like Nulldrifter and Teferi, Time Raveler, the deck aims to maintain board presence and control the flow of the game until it can ultimately win through powerful finishers like Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury or Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer.

The main concept of this deck is to assemble specific combo pieces to generate a powerful win condition without relying on lands. Cards like Hydroelectric Specimen, Goblin Charbelcher, Lotus Bloom, and others are used to create a strong and efficient combo strategy. Additionally, the deck includes disruption and control spells like Disrupting Shoal, Counterspell, and Jwari Disruption to protect the combo pieces and control the game. The deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and disrupt the opponent's game plan to secure victory.

The deck focuses on quickly deploying low-cost artifacts to generate mana and create a board presence, supported by disruptive spells to control the opponent's resources. It aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures and powerful artifact synergies, utilizing cards like Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Urza's Saga to create a consistent and difficult-to-interact-with game plan. The deck's strength lies in its ability to rapidly accumulate advantage and apply pressure without sacrificing resilience or versatility, making it a formidable threat in the fast-paced Modern format.

This deck aims to quickly assemble the combo pieces needed to generate large amounts of mana and storm count to overwhelm the opponent with spells. It uses cards like Ral, Monsoon Mage and Ruby Medallion to reduce the cost of spells, paired with rituals and cantrips to enhance mana generation and card draw. The goal is to cast a large Past in Flames to recur spells from the graveyard and finish off the opponent with a powerful storm turn. Valakut Awakening and Wish provide additional flexibility in finding key cards or reshuffling the deck.

This deck focuses on using creatures with powerful abilities to create a versatile toolbox of options for different situations. By tutoring for specific creatures with spells like Green Sun's Zenith and utilizing sacrifice effects from cards like Yawgmoth, Thran Physician, the deck can control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck also incorporates combo potential with cards like Badgermole Cub and Walking Ballista to generate incremental advantage and pressure the opponent. Overall, it aims to outvalue and outmaneuver the opponent through a combination of creature synergy and resource management.
